top_signals.title: Oil prices hover around $110 as U.S.-Iran tensions simmer after UAE drone strike
top_signals.summary: Oil prices rose on Monday as tensions between the U.S. and Iran remained high following a drone strike which targeted a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates. U.S. President Donald Trump warned that the “clock is ticking” for Iran to accept a deal, as reports suggested that the U.S. and Israel are actively ...

top_signals.title: Dry bulk market update: How Cape & Panamax freight markets are repricing under the new coal trade map
top_signals.summary: Freight firmness Cape Atlantic & Pacific and Panamax Pacific all printing higher rates. Supply is being absorbed. 02 Coal demand reshaped Strait of Hormuz disruption pushed thermal coal demand toward seaborne Asia. Panamax Pacific the immediate beneficiary. 03 Russia → Asia coal corridor April 2026 record high. Russian coal (97.8% of origins) flowing to N.China, ...

top_signals.title: Citi Says 5.5% May Be Next Key Level for 30-Year Treasury Yield
top_signals.summary: Bond traders are zeroing in on 5.5% as the new “round number” for 30-year US Treasury yields as inflation concerns ripple across markets, according to Citigroup Inc.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: ADNOC to increase crude storage in India to 30m barrels
top_signals.summary: The UAE’s energy major ADNOC has announced two strategic collaboration agreements with Indian partners, reinforcing the strong and expanding UAE‑India energy partnership. top_signals.summary: The dry bulk market for Kamsarmax and Panamax bulkers is finding significant boost in the Brazilian soybean export trade. top_signals.title: UK finance workers weigh quitting over back-to-office mandates
top_signals.summary: Financial services workers are weighing quitting their roles if their companies mandate office attendance amid a deepening feud between managers and employees. Nearly six in ten employees in the finance sector said requirements around their office presence is making them more likely to leave their role, according to new research from City recruiter Morgan McKinley.
